explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the system is in NOMINAL mode, it will transition to STANDBY mode in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby).  The G operator ensures this holds globally across all time steps. The implication (->) checks if the condition (bool_exp2) is met before asserting the consequence. The X operator describes the next state. The bool_exp2 represents the current state being NOMINAL and the pilot being in control.  bool_exp3 indicates the next state being STANDBY.

This LTL specification states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will transition to STANDBY mode in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to link the condition (autopilot in NOMINAL and pilot in standby) to the consequent (autopilot transitioning to STANDBY in the next state). The use of X ensures that this happens in the next step, and G ensures it always happens.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to state the condition. The antecedent is state_is_NOMINAL and the consequent is that eventually within N_DURATION time units state_is_STANDBY becomes true. This captures the requirement that the autopilot changes states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby). It uses template 12 because it expresses a reaction to a condition. bool_exp2 represents the condition of being in NOMINAL mode. bool_exp3 represents the transition to STANDBY mode.  The G operator ensures this property holds globally across all time steps. The -> operator means that if the condition is true, the consequence should follow eventually. The F operator denotes the 'eventually' operator, and [0,N_DURATION] limits the amount of time that is checked for the consequence.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby). The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in NOMINAL state (state_is_NOMINAL), and bool_exp3 represents that the pilot is in control (standby). The formula G(bool_exp2 -> F[0, N_DURATION] bool_exp3) ensures that this implication holds globally (at all times).

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state.  The G operator ensures this holds globally (at all times). The -> operator implies that if standby is true, then eventually (F) the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). The index 19 is used as it provides the correct temporal logic to meet the needs of the requirement.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses implication (->) to express the conditional behavior, stating that if the pilot is in control (bool_exp2: standby) then the autopilot will eventually be in STANDBY mode (bool_exp3: state_is_STANDBY) within the specified duration. The G operator ensures that this property holds globally across all time steps.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  The property uses the implication operator (->) to connect the condition (pilot in control) to the consequence (autopilot changing to STANDBY state).  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally across all time steps. The F operator with a bounded interval ensures that transition happens within a given time constraint.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for the NOMINAL state, and bool_exp3 checks for the STANDBY state.  The G operator ensures this property holds globally (at all times), and F[0, N_DURATION] ensures that the transition to STANDBY happens within the specified time window. The implication (->) connects the condition (NOMINAL mode) to the consequence (eventual transition to STANDBY).

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  The property uses a globally enforced implication to capture the "shall" requirement. The antecedent (bool_exp2) checks for the standby condition and the consequent (bool_exp3) verifies that the autopilot reaches the STANDBY state. The G operator ensures this holds for all time. The F operator in the consequent ensures that the STANDBY state is eventually reached within the given time bound.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses the implies operator to ensure that only when the pilot takes control will the transition occur. The choice of template 14 allows for this "eventually" behavior.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 captures when the pilot is in control (standby) and the boolean expression bool_exp3 captures when the autopilot is in STANDBY.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for the current state being NOMINAL, while bool_exp3 checks that the pilot is in control and the autopilot transitions to STANDBY. The G operator ensures this holds globally, and F[0,N_DURATION] allows for a transition within a time window. The use of G and F[0, N_DURATION] makes sure that this happens immediately and eventually without indefinite delay.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to capture this conditional behavior. It uses state_is_NOMINAL and state_is_STANDBY to capture the autopilot's state, and standby to reflect the pilot's control status.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby). The chosen template directly represents this implication with a Globally operator.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby). The property uses the implication operator to express the condition.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks if the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode and bool_exp3 checks if a transition to STANDBY occurs within the specified timeframe.  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally across all time steps.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within the next N_DURATION time steps.  It uses template 14 to express this. bool_exp2 represents the condition of the pilot being in control, and bool_exp3 represents the autopilot being in STANDBY mode.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state.  It uses a template that directly expresses this next-state relationship.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot must be in the STANDBY state. It captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition to STANDBY when the pilot takes control.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state.  The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally, at every point in time.  The F operator indicates that the STANDBY state will eventually be reached after standby becomes true. N_DURATION is set to 1 to allow for a single transition to the standby state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  It uses the implication operator to express the conditional nature of the transition. The formula uses atomic propositions that directly represent the states of the autopilot and the pilot's control status.

This LTL specification states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will transition to STANDBY mode in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby).  The use of X (next) operator ensures the change happens in the next state. Using implication (->) ensures the property only holds when the premise (state_is_NOMINAL & standby) is true.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will transition to STANDBY mode in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby). It uses the atomic propositions state_is_NOMINAL, state_is_STANDBY, and standby to describe the states of the autopilot and the pilot's control status.  The template ensures that the transition happens immediately upon the condition being met.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses G to ensure that this holds globally (in all states), and F[0,2] to ensure that the transition occurs within 2 time units. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  The property uses the implication operator (->) to connect the condition (standby) and the consequence (eventually state_is_STANDBY). The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally (in all states).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to link the condition (autopilot in NOMINAL and pilot in standby) to the consequent (transition to STANDBY). The G operator ensures that this property holds globally, and F[0,2] specifies that the transition must occur within a bounded time interval.  If the pilot is not in control, then no transition is required so the formula is satisfied.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, if the pilot takes control (standby is true), then the autopilot will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units.  The atomic proposition state_is_NOMINAL checks that the autopilot is currently in NOMINAL mode. standby indicates the pilot is in control.  The implication (->) ensures that if the pilot takes control, then the transition to the STANDBY mode (state_is_STANDBY) occurs. The F operator ensures the eventuality and the [0, N_DURATION] interval limits this to N_DURATION time units.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within 2 time units if the pilot is in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks if the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode and not in transition. bool_exp3 checks if the autopilot is in STANDBY mode.  The G operator ensures this holds globally, and F[0,2] specifies that the transition must occur within 2 time units.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses template 14, implying a globally enforced implication. If standby is true, then eventually state_is_STANDBY will become true. The antecedent bool_exp2 captures when the pilot is in control, and the consequent bool_exp3 captures when the autopilot is in the standby state. The use of F[0, N_DURATION] specifies that the transition should happen within a specific timeframe.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses implication (->) to express the conditional relationship between the autopilot being in NOMINAL state and the eventual transition to STANDBY state. It employs the Globally (G) operator to indicate that this condition must hold at all times. Finally, it utilizes the Future (F) operator within a bounded interval to specify that the transition should occur within a specific time window.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state.  It uses the implication operator (->) to connect the pilot's control to the autopilot's state change. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition where the pilot is in control (standby), and bool_exp3 represents the autopilot being in STANDBY state. It uses template 14 because the transition need not happen immediately but rather is required to happen within a bounded amount of time.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next state, the autopilot will be in STANDBY state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, if the pilot takes control (STANDBY is true), then in the next time step the autopilot will transition to STANDBY mode. The property uses the atomic proposition "state_is_NOMINAL" to represent the autopilot being in NOMINAL mode,  "standby" to indicate the pilot is in control, and "state_is_STANDBY" to represent the autopilot being in STANDBY mode.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state. The G operator ensures that this holds globally across all time points.  The -> operator implies the condition, and F[0, N_DURATION] indicates that within the next N_DURATION time units, the state_is_STANDBY becomes true.  We use N_DURATION to set a reasonable time window for the state change to occur and avoid potential indefinite waiting.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication (->) to express the conditional behavior. The antecedent (bool_exp2) checks for the NOMINAL state. The consequent (bool_exp3) checks that the autopilot enters the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units. If the pilot is not in control, the consequent is trivially true.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot must be in the STANDBY state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot takes control.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, if the pilot takes control (standby is true), then in the next state the autopilot will transition to STANDBY.  The property uses the implication operator to express the conditional relationship between the current state (NOMINAL) and the pilot taking control (standby), and then it checks if in the next state the autopilot is indeed in STANDBY mode using the next operator (X). This is a straightforward translation that directly captures the meaning of the natural language statement.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state.  The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1 to allow for a single time step delay for the state transition to occur.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot must change to the STANDBY state.  This captures the natural language requirement by expressing the implication from standby to the next state being standby.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot state will be STANDBY.  It uses the next operator X to express that the state change happens immediately after the pilot takes control. The chosen template perfectly captures the requirement's intent of an immediate state transition upon a condition being met.

This LTL specification states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot takes control (standby). The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks if the autopilot's current mode is NOMINAL (state_is_NOMINAL & !state_is_TRANSITION), and bool_exp3 checks if the pilot is in control (standby) and the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (standby & state_is_STANDBY & !state_is_TRANSITION). The implication (bool_exp2 -> F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) ensures that if the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually be in STANDBY mode when the pilot is in control. The G operator makes this property globally true.

This LTL specification states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  It uses the implication operator (->) to express the conditional behavior. The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition of the pilot being in control (standby), and bool_exp3 represents the autopilot being in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY).  The temporal operator F[0, N_DURATION] indicates that the STANDBY state will be reached within the specified time window.  The outer G operator guarantees this behavior holds globally, at all times.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The property uses the implication operator (->) to express the conditional nature of the transition. The atomic proposition "state_is_NOMINAL" checks the autopilot's current mode, and "state_is_STANDBY" checks whether the autopilot has transitioned to STANDBY. The condition for the transition is given by the "standby" atomic proposition.

G ensures that this property must always hold, and F[0, N_DURATION] means that the transition must occur in at most N_DURATION time steps.

This LTL property states that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), then in the next time step, the autopilot state will change from NOMINAL to STANDBY.  The property uses the atomic proposition "standby" to represent the pilot being in control and uses the implication operator "->" to capture the causal relationship between the pilot taking control and the autopilot changing states. The next operator "X" is used to specify that the state change happens immediately after the pilot takes control.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will change to the STANDBY state. It uses the implication operator to connect the condition (pilot in control) to the consequence (autopilot transitioning to STANDBY). The G operator globally ensures this holds at all times. This LTL property states that whenever the system is in NOMINAL mode, it will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within 1 time unit if the pilot is in control (standby).  The previous attempt was incorrect because it tried to express a state change using a single time step.  This revised translation uses template 19. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in NOMINAL mode (state_is_NOMINAL & !state_is_STANDBY). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY mode (state_is_STANDBY).  The implication (->) ensures the transition only happens when standby is true. N_DURATION is set to 1 to capture the transition within 1 time unit.  The use of X (next) operator is not needed as we only care that the change occurs and the timing is loosely constrained.
